title: How decisions are displayed
question: How should the saved decisions be shown on screen?
rationale: Decisions show as a stage-grouped card grid; clicking one opens it in a focused pop-up. Chosen because it keeps the scan-by-stage review goal but looks designed, where the earlier two-pane list read as plain and the node-graph/3D views drifted from reviewing.

history.reason: The web-of-nodes and 3D views were good for analysis and looked impressive, but got away from the goal of reviewing past decisions. Switched to a grouped review list.
The grouped list showed every decision in full at once and felt text-heavy and overwhelming. Switched to a list-plus-panel view that shows one decision at a time.

history.from: List plus detail panel (one decision at a time)
history.reason: The two-pane list-and-panel read as plain and dated — too close to the original. Switched to an apple.com-style card grid with a focused pop-up per decision, same review goal, more designed.

tradeoffs.note: one script to build the page
strict tree; each item can have only one parent
doesn't picture how decisions link together
plain web page, no outside software, works offline
browse by topic, but shows every decision in full at once

options.label: List plus detail panel (one decision at a time)

tradeoffs.note: left: short list; right: full text of the one you pick
plain offline web page, no outside software
plain two-pane; read as dated, too close to the original
scan cards by stage, then open one decision in a focused pop-up
big type, whitespace, cards in a grid — feels like a real product page
still a plain offline page, opens by double-clicking
